Let me analyze the task. I need to deploy a 3-node Elasticsearch cluster in the `elasticsearch` namespace using native Kubernetes resources (no operators/CRDs). Let me first check the current state of the cluster.

Key requirements:
- 3-node Elasticsearch cluster
- Image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
- TLS secret `es-http-tls` exists (tls.crt, tls.key, ca.crt)
- Password secret `elastic-password` exists with key "password"
- Service named `es-http` for HTTPS access on port 9200
- No CRDs or operators

I'll need to create several resources: a ConfigMap for Elasticsearch settings, a headless Service for pod discovery, a regular Service for external access, a StatefulSet with 3 replicas, and potentially a PodDisruptionBudget. The configuration will need to handle TLS setup since we have the necessary secrets available.

Since Elasticsearch 8.x enables security by default, I'll configure both HTTP and transport TLS using the `es-http-tls` secret, which contains the certificate, key, and CA certificate needed for HTTPS connections.

For the 3-node StatefulSet, I'm setting up the cluster with pods named elasticsearch-0 through elasticsearch-2, a headless service for inter-pod discovery, and a regular service for HTTPS client access. Let me see what infrastructure already exists.

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-headless
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
      targetPort: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
  type: ClusterIP
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${HOSTNAME}
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - elasticsearch-0.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-1.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-2.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - elasticsearch-0
      - elasticsearch-1
      - elasticsearch-2
    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: elasticsearch
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-headless
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
        - name: fix-permissions
          image: busybox:1.36
          command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - name: increase-vm-max-map
          image: busybox:1.36
          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: http
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          env:
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
                    --cacert /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt \
                    -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -qE '^(200)$'
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 6
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "500m"
            limits:
              memory: "2Gi"
              cpu: "1000m"
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 10Gi

The pod is Running but not Ready. Only `elasticsearch-0` is even scheduled - the other two pods haven't started yet. The readiness probe is failing. Let me check the pod logs to see what's happening.

The issue might be:
1. The StatefulSet only creates `elasticsearch-0` first (sequential ordering), and that pod needs to become Ready before creating the next. But since `elasticsearch-0` needs to form a cluster with the other nodes first, it might be in a chicken-and-egg situation.
2. The readiness probe might be too strict - it's checking for `_cluster/health` which might require the cluster to be formed first.
3. There could be a configuration issue with TLS or security.

The logs confirm:
1. elasticsearch-0 is running and trying to discover the other nodes, but they don't exist yet (DNS resolution fails for elasticsearch-1 and elasticsearch-2)
2. The root cause is `podManagementPolicy: OrderedReady` — elasticsearch-0 needs to be Ready before elasticsearch-1 is created, but it can't be Ready without the cluster

The fix is to set `podManagementPolicy: Parallel` so all 3 pods start simultaneously.

Also, I notice the logs show DNS resolution failures for elasticsearch-2, which means the pod for elasticsearch-2 doesn't exist (since only elasticsearch-0 is running).
Since `podManagementPolicy` is immutable on an existing StatefulSet, I'll need to delete and recreate it. I should also reconsider the readiness probe — instead of checking cluster health which requires all nodes up, I'll check if the individual node is responding to connections, like a simple curl to the local port.

The real issue is that with `cluster.initial_master_nodes` specifying all three nodes, Elasticsearch needs a quorum to bootstrap the cluster, so `elasticsearch-0` can't become ready until the other nodes join. I need to think through whether this is actually a blocker or if Elasticsearch 8 handles this differently.

The solution is to change `podManagementPolicy` to `Parallel` so all pods start simultaneously. I could try patching it with kubectl, but `podManagementPolicy` is likely immutable, so I'll need to delete and recreate the StatefulSet while preserving the pods.

With parallel startup, all three pods will begin at once and discover each other through the headless service DNS, which gets populated as soon as pods are created—even before they're fully Ready.

The key issue is that by default, the headless service only publishes DNS records for Ready pods, which creates a chicken-and-egg problem. I need to set `publishNotReadyAddresses: true` on the headless service so that DNS entries for each pod are available immediately, allowing them to discover and form a cluster before becoming Ready. Actually, elasticsearch-0 is running and being resolved fine—the real blocker is the `OrderedReady` policy preventing the other pods from starting. I need to switch to `Parallel` pod management and add `publishNotReadyAddresses: true` to the headless service so all pods can discover each other during startup. My plan is to delete the StatefulSet and update the configuration with these changes, plus simplify the readiness probe to just check the HTTP endpoint.

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-headless
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
      targetPort: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
  type: ClusterIP
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${HOSTNAME}
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - elasticsearch-0.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-1.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-2.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - elasticsearch-0
      - elasticsearch-1
      - elasticsearch-2
    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: elasticsearch
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-headless
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
        - name: fix-permissions
          image: busybox:1.36
          command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - name: increase-vm-max-map
          image: busybox:1.36
          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: http
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          env:
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
                    --cacert /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt \
                    -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -qE '^(200)$'
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 6
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "500m"
            limits:
              memory: "2Gi"
              cpu: "1000m"
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 10Gi

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-headless
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  publishNotReadyAddresses: true
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
      targetPort: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
  type: ClusterIP
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${HOSTNAME}
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - elasticsearch-0.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-1.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-2.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - elasticsearch-0
      - elasticsearch-1
      - elasticsearch-2
    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: elasticsearch
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-headless
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
        - name: fix-permissions
          image: busybox:1.36
          command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - name: increase-vm-max-map
          image: busybox:1.36
          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: http
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          env:
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
                    --cacert /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt \
                    -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -qE '^(200)$'
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 6
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "500m"
            limits:
              memory: "2Gi"
              cpu: "1000m"
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 10Gi
